title: Rust: Missing unified hybrid Router abstraction (spec 7.2)
description: Python has Router class (599 LoC) combining address classification, gradient lookup, RPL/LOADng/GPSR routing decisions, pending packet queue, and DTN buffer. Rust has individual protocol implementations (announce.rs, loadng.rs, rpl/) but no unified orchestration layer. Reference: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py
close_reason: Implemented: Added unified HybridRouter abstraction (hybrid.rs) with address classification (LinkLocal/MeshLocal/External), route decision logic dispatching to gradient table/RPL/LOADng, pending packet queue, GPSR fallback, and mesh prefix configuration. Also added GradientTable (gradient.rs) with LRU eviction, RFC 1982 seq_num comparison, and source priority. 27 new tests covering routing decisions, gradient updates, pending queue, and GPSR. Matches Python Router class structure from spec 7.2.
